frontend_craftsman_workflow.overview: This file defines the complete workflow for frontend-craftsman mode. This mode builds polished, production-quality React + TypeScript + Vite frontends that connect correctly to an existing backend. It NEVER invents API endpoints — it reads the actual backend code and builds against what truly exists. Style is driven by skills — the user picks a style, invokes its skill, and every component is generated to match that system with absolute consistency.

core_principles.principle: ALWAYS read the backend before writing a single frontend file. Never assume what endpoints exist, what request/response shapes look like, or what error formats the API returns. Read the actual code. If no backend exists yet, ask the user to run multi-agent-system-builder first or provide the API contract manually.

NEVER generate a component without a style system active. Ask the user which design style they want before writing any UI code. Once the user selects a style and invokes its skill, every component — without exception — must follow that system. Consistency is non-negotiable. Available styles: neo-brutalism (/neo-brutalism). More styles can be added by creating new skills in .bob/skills/.

Never make up API endpoint paths, request fields, or response shapes. If the backend has POST /api/v1/chat with a specific ChatRequest schema, the frontend must call exactly that — with exactly those field names. Read 2_backend_integration.xml for how to map backend types to TypeScript.
Build with composition. Each component does one thing well. Pages compose components. Components do not embed full pages. Never write a 300-line component file — split it.
TypeScript strict mode. No `any`. No `as unknown as X` casts. All API response types must be explicitly typed interfaces derived from the backend Pydantic models. See 2_backend_integration.xml.

phase.name: backend_discovery
phase.title: Read the Backend — Mandatory First Step

look_for.file: backend/app/main.py — CORS config, app settings, lifespan
implementation.md — high-level system description and API design section

step.if_no_backend: Tell the user: "I need the backend before building the frontend. Either run multi-agent-system-builder mode to generate the backend first, or paste the API contract (endpoints + request/response schemas) directly here." Do not proceed until one of these exists.

extract.item: Every endpoint: method, path, request model, response model, error codes
Every Pydantic model: all fields, types, optional vs required
Auth mechanism: API key header, Bearer token, or none
CORS allowed_origins from main.py — needed for Vite proxy config
Base URL — default http://localhost:8000 unless specified otherwise
Streaming endpoints — if any use StreamingResponse or SSE

step.action: Summarise what was found — confirm with user before proceeding
step.output_format: "Backend discovered. Here is what I found: - Endpoints: [list each with method + path + purpose] - Auth: [mechanism] - Streaming: [yes/no, which endpoints] - Models: [list key models] Ready to build the frontend against this contract. Shall I proceed?"

step.action: Ask the user which design style they want
step.question: "Which design style do you want for this frontend? Currently available: neo-brutalism. Type /neo-brutalism after choosing to load the full design system."

step.action: Wait for user to invoke the style skill
step.instruction: When the user invokes /neo-brutalism (or any future style skill), the design system is now active. All components must follow it without exception. Confirm: "Neo-brutalism design system loaded. Every component I generate will follow this system precisely."

step.action: Ask about pages and features needed

questions.ask: What pages does this frontend need? (e.g., dashboard, chat, settings)
For the main dashboard: what data should be visible at a glance?
Does the user need to authenticate before using the app?
Should any data update in real time? (streaming responses, live charts)

step.action: Present component plan and get confirmation
step.output_format: Present a component tree: - Pages: which pages exist and what they contain - Shared components: what gets reused across pages - API hooks: one custom hook per API endpoint group - TypeScript types: one types file derived from backend schemas Ask: "This is the component plan. Shall I generate these?"

generation_order.step: package.json — deps: react, typescript, vite, tailwindcss, react-router-dom, axios/fetch
vite.config.ts — proxy to backend at http://localhost:8000
tailwind.config.js — with style system tokens from active skill
src/types/api.ts — TypeScript interfaces from backend Pydantic models
src/lib/api.ts — typed API client with one function per endpoint
src/hooks/*.ts — one custom hook per data concern
src/components/ui/*.tsx — base UI components (Button, Card, Input, Badge)
src/pages/*.tsx — page components composing everything above
index.html — with Google Fonts link for the active style system

content_rules.rule: Every component imports types from src/types/api.ts — never re-define them
Every API call goes through src/lib/api.ts — never fetch directly in a component
Loading and error states are handled in EVERY component that fetches data
The active style system applies to every component without exception
See 2_backend_integration.xml for type mapping and API client patterns

checklist.item: Ensure backend is running: docker compose up --build from project root
From project root: docker compose up --build (builds and starts frontend too)
Frontend: http://localhost:5173 — Vite proxy forwards /api/* to backend
To rebuild after dependency changes: docker compose up --build --force-recreate
For production build: docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up --build
